name: Elvis Presley
description: The King of Rock 'n' Roll — life, music, and films

questions.q: What is Elvis Presley's middle name?

source.notes: Born Elvis Aaron Presley in 1935 (sometimes spelled 'Aron' on early documents).

questions.q: What is the name of Elvis Presley's famous mansion and estate in Memphis?

source.notes: Elvis bought Graceland in 1957; it opened to the public in 1982.

questions.q: Elvis Presley earned his 'King' nickname in which genre of music?

source.notes: Often called simply 'The King' or 'The King of Rock and Roll'.

questions.q: What is the name of Elvis's only child, born in 1968?

source.notes: Born February 1, 1968 to Elvis and Priscilla Presley.

questions.q: In which branch of the U.S. military did Elvis Presley serve from 1958 to 1960?

source.notes: Drafted as a private; served with the 3rd Armored Division in West Germany.

questions.q: Graceland is located in which U.S. state?

source.notes: Located in Memphis, Tennessee.

questions.q: Lisa Marie Presley was famously married for less than two years to which pop superstar?

source.notes: Married May 1994 to January 1996.

questions.q: Elvis's '68 Comeback Special, which revived his live-performance career, aired on NBC in which year?

source.notes: Simply titled 'Elvis'; aired December 3, 1968.

questions.q: Who managed Elvis Presley's career for more than two decades?

source.notes: Dutch-born Andreas Cornelis van Kuijk; managed Elvis 1956–1977.

questions.q: At which Memphis record label did Elvis Presley record his first singles in 1954?

source.notes: Sam Phillips' label; first recording was 'That's All Right' in July 1954.

questions.q: Which U.S. President did Elvis famously meet at the White House in December 1970?

source.notes: The photograph of the two is among the most requested from the U.S. National Archives.

questions.q: Which British reggae band took a cover of Elvis's 'Can't Help Falling in Love' to #1 in the UK in 1993?

source.notes: Featured in the film 'Sliver' (1993).

questions.q: Which 1956 Elvis single was the first rock 'n' roll record to sell over a million copies?

source.notes: Elvis's first single for RCA; #1 on Billboard for 7 weeks in 1956.

questions.q: What was the title of Elvis Presley's first feature film, released in 1956?

source.notes: A Civil War-era drama; Elvis played Clint Reno.

questions.q: In which Elvis film from 1964 did he play a race-car driver named Lucky Jackson?

source.notes: Co-starred Ann-Margret.

questions.q: Which variety show host featured Elvis three times in 1956–57, including one where cameras famously only showed him from the waist up?

source.notes: His third appearance (January 1957) is the one with the waist-up restriction.

questions.q: Which major record label signed Elvis in late 1955 and released nearly all his subsequent albums?

source.notes: Sun Records sold Elvis's contract to RCA for $35,000 in November 1955.

questions.q: The double-A-side 'Don't Be Cruel / Hound Dog' topped the Billboard chart for how many consecutive weeks in 1956 — then a record?

source.notes: 11 weeks at #1; stood as the longest run at the top for decades.

questions.q: In what year was Elvis Presley inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame as an inaugural member?

source.notes: Part of the Hall's first induction class, January 23, 1986.

questions.q: Elvis's 1973 satellite-broadcast concert 'Aloha from Elvis' was transmitted from which U.S. location?

source.notes: Broadcast live via satellite from Honolulu International Center on January 14, 1973.

questions.q: In what year did the U.S. Postal Service issue its famous Elvis Presley commemorative stamp?

source.notes: The public voted between a 'young Elvis' and 'old Elvis' design; young Elvis won.

questions.q: Elvis Presley's identical twin brother, stillborn in 1935, was named what?

source.notes: Jesse Garon Presley was delivered stillborn 35 minutes before Elvis on January 8, 1935.
